description.task: Implement a BFSTraversal class that uses a Queue to perform BFS on a graph represented as an adjacency list. Return nodes grouped by their BFS level (distance from source).
instructions: Inside the while loop, process exactly levelSize nodes from queue
For each dequeued node: add it to the current level list; for each unvisited neighbor: mark visited, enqueue
After processing levelSize nodes, add level to levels
BFS from node 1: Level 0=[1], Level 1=[2,3], Level 2=[4,5,6]
Use graph.getOrDefault(node, Collections.emptyList()) for neighbor lookup

description.task: Implement a TaskScheduler that simulates Round-Robin CPU scheduling. Each task has a name and burst time. The scheduler processes tasks in FIFO order, giving each up to 'quantum' time units per turn. Print when each task starts, preempts, and completes.
instructions: When task.burstTime > 0 after running: re-enqueue the task with its remaining burst time
The main scheduling loop logic and output prints are already provided
P1=10, P2=4, P3=6 with quantum=4: P1 runs twice (4+4+2), P2 once (4), P3 twice (4+2)
description.expectedOutput:
t=0: P1 runs for 4 units
t=4: P2 runs for 4 units
t=8: P2 DONE
t=8: P3 runs for 4 units
t=12: P1 runs for 4 units
t=16: P3 runs for 2 units
t=18: P3 DONE
t=18: P1 runs for 2 units
t=20: P1 DONE

description.task: Implement a BinaryPrinter class that uses a Queue to generate binary representations of numbers from 1 to n. Start with '1'; for each dequeued string, append '0' for the left child and '1' for the right child, enqueue both.
instructions: Dequeue front string, add to result list
Enqueue front+"0" (append zero) and front+"1" (append one)
This BFS-based approach generates binary strings level-order (shorter before longer)
